data.title: Clean Harbors Prices $745 Million Notes Offering
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Clean Harbors (CLH) said late Thursday it has priced a private placement of $745 million of 5.75% senior notes due 2033 at 100% of par value.

The private placement was downsized from the previously set amount of $845 million as a result of a $100 million increase in a new term loan credit facility, the company said.

Closing of the sale is expected on or about Oct. 9.

Net proceeds will be used for debt repayments and to pay related fees and expenses, the company said.

data.title: Indonesia's finance minister plans meetings with central bank to synchronise policy
data.description:
Jakarta, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Indonesia's finance minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa on Friday said he would hold regular meetings with central bank officials to synchronise policies.

(Reporting by Stefanno Sulaiman; Writing by Gayatri Suroyo; Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: Trump says South Korea, Japan will pay billions 'upfront' in investment
data.description:
SEOUL/TOKYO (Reuters) -U.S. President Donald Trump insisted that South Korea would provide billions of dollars in investments "upfront", despite Seoul's contention that it would be plunged into a financial crisis if it met the U.S. demands without safeguards.

Trump's remarks contradict South Korea's understanding of its trade deal with the United States, however, a government official told Reuters, speaking on condition of anonymity because of the sensitivity of the talks.

"We have never thought about making the investments in lump sum," the South Korean official added, adding that both South Korea and Japan intended to provide financing for projects only after a "capital call" made by the United States.

South Korea, which pledged $350 billion toward U.S. projects in July, has balked at U.S. demands for control over the funds and South Korean officials say talks to formalise their trade deal are at a deadlock.

Trump formalised a trade deal with Japan this month, lowering tariffs on imports of its automobiles and other products in return for $550 billion of its investment in U.S. projects, and U.S. officials have pressed Seoul to follow suit.

"We have in Japan it's $550 billion, South Korea's $350 billion. That's upfront," Trump told reporters on Thursday in the Oval Office, as he touted the amount of money he said his sweeping tariffs have brought in.

Trump's comments came as political doubts have increasingly dogged his trade talks with South Korea, spooking investors who now worry Seoul may end up with a raw deal or perhaps none at all.

Both Japan and South Korea have said they will make investments based on U.S. projects, rather than paying the total of $900 billion upfront.

A memorandum of understanding on Japan's $550 billion investment agreed with the United States in September also made no mention of "upfront" payment of the funds.

It says the investments should be made 'from time to time' until the end of Trump's term in January 2029. Under its deal, Tokyo agreed to transfer money within 45 days after the U.S. selected a project.

Japanese officials did not comment on Trump's "upfront" remarks on Friday.

South Korea has also said it cannot afford to make large cash investments. Last week President Lee Jae Myung told Reuters that without safeguards such as a currency swap, South Korea's economy could be plunged into crisis.

A second South Korean government official declined to comment on Trump's remarks, but reiterated that talks with the United States were based on the principle that the deal should meet national interests and be commercially feasible.

Analysts say a currency swap is unlikely, and South Korean negotiators are pushing for most of the funds to be in the form of loans, rather than direct investment.

They are also pressing Washington for mechanisms to ensure that the projects are commercially viable.

(Reporting by Jihoon Lee, Cynthia Kim, Hyunjoo Jin in Seoul and John Geddie in Tokyo; Writing by Josh Smith; Editing by Sam Holmes and Clarence Fernandez)

data.title: Chunghwa Precision Test Tech August net profit up 314% Y/Y
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) - Chunghwa Precision Test Tech Co Ltd:

  • August net profit T$89 million ($2.9 mln), +314% Y/Y
  • Disclosure of the result is at request of the Taipei Exchange amid recent share price volatility

(Reporting by Faith Hung)

data.title: Iran says deal with IAEA hinges on lack of hostile action, including sanctions snapback
data.description:
DUBAI (Reuters) -Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said on Friday that Tehran would scrap an agreement to let the U.N. watchdog inspect its nuclear sites if Western powers reinstated U.N. sanctions.

A set of U.N. sanctions on Iran are due to be reimposed at 0000 GMT on Saturday after Britain, France and Germany accused Tehran of violating a 2015 deal with major powers aimed at preventing it from developing a nuclear weapon.

The International Atomic Energy Agency had been trying to rebuild cooperation with Tehran and resume inspections of its nuclear facilities after Israel and the United States bombed the sites in June.

But in a post on Telegram, Araqchi said a deal signed in Egypt this month "will remain valid as long as no hostile action is taken against Iran, including the reinstatement of United Nations Security Council resolutions that have been annulled".

He added: "Otherwise, the Islamic Republic of Iran will consider its practical commitments terminated."

The Security Council is to vote on Friday on a resolution proposed by Russia and China to delay the reimposition of sanctions under the so-called "snapback mechanism" for six months, but diplomats said it was unlikely to pass.

The mechanism, enshrined in Security Council Resolution 2231, allows any party to the 2015 agreement with the U.S., Russia, China, Britain, France and Germany to unilaterally restore the U.N. sanctions that were suspended by the deal.

Tehran has long denounced the provision as a political weapon that undermines diplomacy.

(Reporting by Jana Choukeir and Ahmed Elimam; Editing by Toby Chopra and Kevin Liffey)

data.title: Boeing Receives Orders From Turkish Airlines, Norwegian Group
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Boeing (BA) said Friday it has received aircraft orders from Turkish Airlines and the Norwegian Group.

The deal with Turkish Airlines includes a firm order for up to 75 787 Dreamliners and commitments for up to 150 737 MAX jets, the company said.

Boeing expects the firm order to support over 123,000 jobs across the US.

In a separate statement, Boeing said the Norwegian Group has placed an order for 30 737-8 airplanes.

data.title: Panasonic: to delist shares on Nagoya exchange
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) - Panasonic Holdings Corp:

  • Panasonic: to delist shares on Nagoya Stock Exchange

(Reporting by Kiyoshi Takenaka)

data.title: AT&T Keeps Quarterly Dividend of $0.2775 per Share, Payable Nov. 3 to Shareholders as of Oct. 10
data.description: (MT Newswires) --

data.title: Ecb to conduct new digital euro experiments next year
data.description:
FRANKFURT (Reuters) -The European Central Bank said on Friday it would conduct new experiments next year about what could be achieved through a digital euro, in a further step for a project it sees as key for preserving the euro zone's financial autonomy from the United States.

The ECB said this year's experiments with the private sector showed the digital euro - an electronic wallet backed by the central bank - could facilitate, for example, automated payments on public transport or certain reimbursements.

(Reporting By Francesco Canepa; Editing by Alison Williams)

data.title: Noba Bank Shares Surge 27 Percent in Nasdaq Stockholm Debut
data.description:
Specialist lender Noba Bank saw its shares soar in Friday's debut trading on Nasdaq Stockholm. The stock is listed on the Large Cap segment under the ticker NOBA.

The share offering was oversubscribed multiple times, with the company attracting more than 33,000 shareholders. The subscription price was set at SEK 70 per share, corresponding to a total market capitalization of SEK 35 billion for all shares in the company. The offering consisted of existing shares sold by private equity firm Nordic Capital and Finnish insurance group Sampo.

The offering comprised 108.70 million existing shares, along with an over-allotment option for an additional 16.30 million existing shares, bringing the total to a maximum of 125 million shares.

The value of the offering amounts to SEK 7.6 billion, rising to SEK 8.75 billion if the over-allotment option is fully exercised.

Among the major institutional investors subscribing to the offering were OP Cooperative, DNB Asset Management, and Handelsbanken Fonder. Together, they acquired shares worth nearly SEK 3.2 billion.

So far today, the share has traded as high as SEK 90 and as low as SEK 83.11. As of 09:30, the latest transaction was at SEK 88.89, representing a 27 percent increase compared to the subscription price. Shares worth SEK 680 million have changed hands so far today. Based on the latest price, the company's market capitalization stands at SEK 44.4 billion.

data.title: Shenma Industry to Boost Stake in Subsidiary for 200 Million Yuan
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Shenma Industry (SHA:600810) plans to purchase a 2.16% stake in subsidiary Henan Shenma Nylon Chemical from Jinshi Manufacturing Transformation and Upgrading New Materials Fund for 200 million yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The stake is equivalent to about 94.6 million shares, the filing said.

Following the transaction, Shenma's shareholding in the subsidiary will increase to 74.22% from 72.06% while Jinshi Fund's stake will fall to 3.23% from 5.39%, the filing said.

Shares closed 10% higher on Friday.

data.title: Eni and five other oil companies fined by Italian competition watchdog
data.description:
ROME (Reuters) -Italy's antitrust regulator has fined energy group Eni and five other oil companies operating in the country for practices restricting fair competition in the sale of fuel for trucks, the watchdog said on Friday.

The regulator said that fines totalling more than 936 million euros ($1.09 billion) had been levied against Eni and ExxonMobil-owned Esso, plus Ip, Q8, Saras and Tamoil.

The companies were found to have operated as a cartel between January 2020 and June 2023 to set the price of the biofuel component contributing to the overall cost of fuel, the watchdog said in a statement. The value of this important price component rose from about 20 euros per cubic metre in 2019 to about 60 euros in 2023, it said.

The body added that what it described as a "complex investigation" was launched after receiving a tip-off from a whistleblower.

The authority imposed fines of 336 million euros on Eni, 173 million euros on Q8, 164 million euros on Ip, 129 million euros on Esso, 91 million euros on Tamoil and 44 million euros on Saras.

Eni and IP did not respond immediately to requests for comment and Reuters was unable to contact the other companies immediately.

($1 = 0.8562 euros)

(Reporting by Gavin JonesEditing by David Goodman)

data.title: Turkish Airlines completes deal to buy 225 Boeing planes after Erdogan-Trump talks
data.description:
ISTANBUL (Reuters) -Turkish Airlines said on Friday it would order 75 Boeing 787 aircraft and had completed negotiations to buy 150 737 MAX planes, subject to engine talks, unveiling a long-awaited deal after a meeting between the two countries' leaders.

President Tayyip Erdogan on Thursday met U.S. President Donald Trump, who said he believed Turkey would agree to his request to stop buying Russian oil and that he may lift U.S. sanctions on Ankara so it can buy American F-35 jets.

Trump said ahead of the talks that the Boeing order would also be under discussion.

LOOKING TO BOOST ITS GLOBAL REACH

Turkish Airlines is looking to boost its global reach and recently bought a minority stake in Spain's Air Europa, outlasting its European rivals Lufthansa and Air France-KLM.

In a statement to the Istanbul stock exchange, the carrier said it decided to purchase 75 wide-body B787-9 and B787-10 models from U.S.-based Boeing, consisting of 50 firm and 25 option orders.

Deliveries are scheduled between 2029 and 2034.

Negotiations were ongoing with Rolls-Royce and GE Aerospace regarding engines, spare engines and engine maintenance services for the planes, it said.

Turkish Airlines also said it completed negotiations with Boeing on the purchase of another 150 aircraft, consisting of 100 firm and 50 option orders for narrow-body 737-8/10 MAX models.

It said orders for the 737-8/10 MAX aircraft would be placed subject to the successful conclusion of discussions with engine manufacturer CFM International.

Turkish Airlines shares were up 0.15% at 328.75 lira in Istanbul in early Friday trade.

EXPANSION PLANS

The deal has been a long time in the making, with the company's chairman having revealed details on the planned purchase of 225 aircraft in June 2024.

"With these orders, our entire fleet aims to consist of new-generation aircraft by 2035, thereby strengthening operational efficiency and supporting an average annual growth rate of around 6%," the company said in Friday's statement.

According to its 2023-2033 strategic plan, Turkish Airlines aims to expand its fleet to over 800 aircraft by 2033. It had 485 aircraft by the end of June, according to its latest presentation.

In line with its strategic plan, the carrier said in May 2023 that it had started talks with the manufacturers in order to procure around 600 aircraft. It placed an order for 355 Airbus planes in December 2023.

(Additional reporting by Ceyda Caglayan; Editing by Kim Coghill, Jonathan Spicer and Alex Richardson)

By Can Sezer and Daren Butler

data.title: Digital bank NOBA soars 27% in Stockholm IPO debut
data.description:
OSLO (Reuters) -The share price of digital banking group NOBA rose 27% on its first day of trading on Friday following an initial public offering on the Stockholm bourse, valuing the company at 44.5 billion Swedish crowns ($4.71 billion).

NOBA, controlled by private equity firm Nordic Capital and Finnish insurer Sampo, was the second major Swedish financial services group to go public this year, with buy now, pay later lender Klarna making its New York debut this month.

NOBA's shares traded at 88.95 Swedish crowns at 0716 GMT, up from 70 crowns in the IPO which the bookrunners earlier this week said had been multiple times oversubscribed by investors.

NOBA operates across the Nordic region under the brands Nordax Bank, Bank Norwegian and Svensk Hypotekspension, and offers credit cards in Germany as well as deposit products in Germany, Spain, the Netherlands and Ireland.

($1 = 9.4429 Swedish crowns)

(Reporting by Terje Solsvik, editing by Stine Jacobsen)

data.title: Boeing and Norwegian Group sign order for 30 737-8 aircraft
data.description:
Boeing and Norwegian Group announced an order for 30 737-8 aircraft. The agreement represents the group's first direct order from Boeing since 2017 and brings its 737 MAX order book to 80 aircraft.

Norwegian has primarily operated Boeing single-aisle aircraft since its first order for the next-generation 737-800 in 2007.

It was the first European airline to take delivery of the 737 MAX in 2017 and the first to operate the 737-8 on transatlantic routes between Europe and the United States.

The agreement signed today for 30 additional 737-8s will support its ambition to become the airline of choice in Scandinavia, providing the flexibility to expand in Europe and beyond, said Brad McMullen, senior vice president of commercial sales and marketing at Boeing.

data.title: China Steel August pretax profit T$1.29 billion
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) - China Steel Corp:

  • August pretax profit T$1.29 billion ($42.3 mln), August revenue T$24.74 billion

(Reporting by Faith Hung)

data.title: DB Insurance to Acquire US-based Specialty Insurer Fortegra Group for $1.65 Billion
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- South Korean insurer DB Insurance (KRX:005830) will acquire US-based specialty insurer Fortegra Group for $1.65 billion, marking the largest US deal by a Korean non-life insurer, Bloomberg reported Friday.

The all-cash acquisition is being advised by Goldman Sachs for DB Insurance and Barclays and BofA for Fortegra. The deal is pending Fortegra's parent company, Tiptree's (NASDAQ:TIPT) approval, and is expected to close by mid-2026, the report said.

The Fortegra Group specializes in specialty insurance and has operations across the US and Europe.

Shares of DB Insurance fell nearly 1% at market close.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: RUBBER-Japan futures slip on improving weather, weak China auto sales
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) -

* Japanese rubber futures fell on Friday but closed the week higher as concerns over supply disruptions caused by recent severe weather in key producing regions supported prices, although weak China auto sales capped gains.

* The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for March delivery was down 1.9 yen, or 0.61%, at 310.2 yen ($2.07) per kg.

* Still, the contract ended the week up 1.04%.

* The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for January delivery lost 160 yuan, or 1.02%, to 15,470 yuan ($2,168.40) per metric ton.

* The most-active November butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE fell 125 yuan, or 1.08%, to 11,430 yuan per ton.

* Top Chinese automaker BYD has reduced prices on select variants of its best-selling models due to declining sales.

* This comes after BYD's weakest annual growth in five years, while the Chinese government has moved to curb excessive price competition in the auto market.

* Automobile sales could influence the intensity of automobile manufacturing, which involves using rubber-made tyres.

* Lower automobile prices, driven by fierce competition, pressure rubber tyre prices.

* Despite temporary typhoon-related disruptions in the Western Pacific, rainfall in Southeast Asia is easing, leading to an expected pickup in production, said Chinese financial information website Jintou.

* The yen traded at an eight-week low after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a new raft of tariffs.

* A weaker Japanese currency makes yen-denominated assets more affordable to overseas buyers.

* Top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency warned of heavy rains and accumulations from September 26-30.

* The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for October delivery last traded at 174 U.S. cents per kg, up 0.5%. ($1 = 149.7300 yen) ($1 = 7.1343 Chinese yuan) (Reporting by Lucas Liew; Editing by Subhranshu Sahu and Sonia Cheema)

data.title: China issues plan to steady growth in petrochemical and chemical industry
data.description:
Beijing, Sept 26 (Reuters) - China's Industry Ministry released a plan on Friday that aims to stabilise growth in its petrochemical and chemical industry, by targeting average annual growth of more than 5% in value added by the sector from 2025 to 2026.

It also looked to control new refining capacity, regulate the pace of new ethylene and paraxylene capacity and prevent overcapacity risks in the coal-to-methanol industry.

(Reporting by Beijing Newsroom; Editing by Clarence Fernandez)

data.title: Indonesia tobacco excise tax to stay unchanged in 2026
data.description:
Jakarta, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Indonesia will keep its excise tax rates on tobacco products unchanged next year, after considering the impact such taxes have on employment in the tobacco industry, Finance minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa told a press conference on Friday.

(Reporting by Stefanno Sulaiman; Writing by Gayatri Suroyo; Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: Rivian Automotive's Delivery Vans Under Preliminary Probe Over Possible Seat Belt Failures
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- (Updates with Rivian's response in the fourth paragraph.)

Rivian Automotive (RIVN) electric delivery vans are under a preliminary evaluation by the US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration's Office of Defects Investigation over "potential failures" of the driver's front outboard seat belt anchorage system, the regulator said this week.

According to the regulator, an estimated 17,198 Rivian delivery vans produced between 2022 and 2023 are under scrutiny after the agency received six vehicle owner questionnaires reporting "multiple instances" in which the seat belt cable "frays, breaks, and/or unravels," leaving the occupants unrestrained in the event of a collision.

The investigation will focus on the integrity of the seat belt assembly, method of installation and potential design or manufacturing changes and deficiencies, the regulator said.

"We look forward to cooperating with NHTSA on its investigation," Rivian said in an emailed statement.

data.title: Reckitt Benckiser to Launch Second Tranche of GBP1 Billion Share Buyback
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Reckitt Benckiser Group (RKT.L) plans to launch the second 250 million-pound-sterling tranche of its 2025 share repurchase program worth 1 billion pounds.

The second tranche, set to run until Jan. 30, 2026, will commence following the expected completion of the first tranche in October 2025, according to a Friday release. BNP Paribas will manage the program on the London bourse and other multilateral trading facilities on behalf of the British consumer goods company.

All repurchased shares will be held in treasury for cancellation until the completion of the second tranche.

data.title: Dragon Shield Announces New Licensing Agreement with Hasbro and Wizards of the Coast to Produce Official MAGIC: THE GATHERING Gaming Accessories
data.description:
Dragon Shield announced a new collaboration with Hasbro and its Wizards of the Coast division to produce licensed trading card game (TCG) accessories for MAGIC: THE GATHERING, focusing on classic/evergreen, iconic imagery from the world's most popular trading card game. The first products under the collaboration will debut in two distinct series that honor MAGIC's enduring legacy:
The Legendary Series will feature some of the most iconic legendary creatures from MAGIC: THE GATHERING's history. Appropriately, the first Legendary Series sleeves will feature The Ur-Dragon as Dual Matte Art sleeves ? a fan-favorite that embodies the timeless power and imagination the series seeks to honor.
The Landfall Series draws inspiration from the landscapes that form the foundation of MAGIC: THE GATHERING. To launch the series, Dragon Shield will release a series of five Dual Matte Art sleeves featuring the five Tarkir: Dragonstorm fetch lands in their showcase forms, celebrating both the artistry and the gameplay significance that lands bring to MAGIC.

data.title: Switzerland says analysing impact of Trump tariff announcements
data.description:
Zurich, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Switzerland said on Friday that government departments are analysing the potential impact of pharmaceutical tariff measures announced by U.S. President Donald Trump.

Trump said on Thursday that the U.S. will impose a 100% tariff on imports of branded or patented pharmaceutical products from October 1, unless a pharmaceutical company is building a manufacturing plant in the U.S.

The Swiss economy ministry said in a statement that the relevant departments are analysing the impact of the measures together with the relevant stakeholders. It noted that it did not have details of the measures announced.

(Reporting by Dave Graham, editing by Thomas Seythal)

data.title: BMW to recall over 196,000 US vehicles due to engine starter defect
data.description:
(Reuters) -BMW is recalling over 196,000 vehicles in the U.S. as the engine starter relay may corrode, leading to overheat and short circuit, increasing the risk of a fire, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Friday.

(Reporting by Ruchika Khanna in Bengaluru; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

data.title: China buys most of 40 Argentina soy cargoes this week for Nov-Dec, traders say
data.description:
BEIJING/SINGAPORE, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Around 40 Argentine soybean cargoes were registered for export in November and December during this week's export tax suspension, mostly headed to China, two traders told Reuters, in purchases that directly eat into the prime U.S. marketing season.

A total of 2.66 million tons of soybeans were registered for November and December, accounting for more than 50% of the 5.1 million tons of total volume booked for all months cited by Argentine officials during the tax-free window, the two Asian traders said on Friday. The buying frenzy by Chinese importers this week was a fresh blow for U.S. soybean farmers, who have been shut out of exports to top market China during the current harvest season as trade war tariffs make their beans prohibitively expensive for Chinese buyers.

"If you look at purchases for November and December, China has further reduced its need for U.S. soybeans by booking Argentinian cargoes," said an oilseed trader at an international firm which is among the buyers. Reuters on Wednesday reported that around 20 cargoes were swiftly booked by China after the tax suspension was announced - a short-lived move by Argentina's government to attract up to $7 billion in sales to boost U.S. dollar reserves and stabilise the struggling peso. The tax break was quickly rolled back after the sales cap was reached.

According to the United States Department of Agriculture (USDA), as of September 11, China had not bought any U.S. soybean cargoes from its autumn harvest.

The critical U.S. marketing window runs from September through January. (Reporting by Ella Cao in Beijing and Naveen Thukral in Singapore; Editing by Tony Munroe and Hugh Lawson)

data.title: South Korea's Business Sentiment Records Second Month of Improvement in September
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- The composite business sentiment index in all industries in South Korea increased 0.6 points to 91.6 in September from August, while the outlook for the next month declined by 3.3 points to 88.5, according to data released by the Bank of Korea on Friday.

The seasonally adjusted composite business sentiment index for the manufacturing sector was up 0.1 points to 93.4, while the outlook for the following month decreased by 2.7 points to 89.4, the central bank said.

For the non-manufacturing sector, the seasonally adjusted composite business sentiment index increased 1.1 points to 90.5, while the outlook for the following month also declined by 3.6 points to 87.9.

Meanwhile, the economic sentiment index, a composite of the business survey and consumer survey indices, went down 3.3 points to 91.3.

data.title: Indonesia's Top Court Reverses Acquittal of Wilmar Subsidiaries in Cooking-Oil Case
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Wilmar International (SGX:F34) said Indonesia's Supreme Court has overturned earlier acquittals of its five subsidiaries in a case over alleged losses to the state during the 2021 cooking-oil shortage, according to a Thursday filing with the Singaporean bourse.

Shares of the company were down over 1% in Friday trading.

The Attorney General's Office had appealed the verdict, seeking damages of about 12.3 trillion rupiah.

Wilmar confirmed the company had earlier placed a security deposit of 11.9 trillion rupiah but maintains its units acted in compliance with regulations and in good faith.

The final award amount will be released once the court issues its formal judgment.

data.title: China Petroleum Engineering Subsidiary Snags More Than $2.5 Billion Seawater Pipeline Project in Iraq
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Petroleum Engineering (SHA:600339) subsidiary China Petroleum Pipeline Engineering signed an engineering, procurement, and construction contract with Iraq's Basra Oil for a $2.52 billion seawater pipeline project, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The contract is expected to last 54 months, with 42 months for the design, procurement, construction, and commissioning phase, and 12 months for the operation, maintenance, and training phase, the filing said.

Shares closed 2% lower on Friday.

data.title: Boeing: Turkish Airlines orders 75 Boeing 787 Dreamliners and commits to purchasing 737 MAX aircraft
data.description:
Boeing and Turkish Airlines have announced a firm order for up to 75 Boeing 787 Dreamliners.

This is the largest acquisition of Boeing widebody aircraft ever made by the national airline.

The agreement includes 35 787-9 aircraft, 15 larger 787-10 aircraft, and options for 25 Boeing 787 Dreamliners.

The airline also announced its intention to purchase up to 150 additional 737 MAX aircraft, which will be its largest order for Boeing single-aisle aircraft once finalized.

"We look forward to continuing to support Turkish Airlines as it expands its operations," said Stephanie Pope, president and CEO of Boeing Commercial Airplanes.

data.title: Goldman Sachs anticipates a recovery in mergers and acquisitions, driven by sector concentration
data.description:
Goldman Sachs is increasingly optimistic about the mergers and acquisitions (M&A) market, driven by the concentration of several industries and an acceleration of private equity-backed transactions, according to its president, John Waldron.

"I think you're going to see more companies, CEOs, and boards taking action, much like what we're seeing in the railroad industry," he said at a conference organized by the Financial Times in New York.

"I would say we are more than optimistic. The growing interest we are seeing in discussions is really making us more confident," he added.

Last July, Union Pacific announced the acquisition of its competitor Norfolk Southern for $85bn, with the aim of creating the first US rail operator covering the entire country, from the East Coast to the West Coast. This deal could redefine freight logistics in the US, from grain to vehicles.

Globally, mergers and acquisitions totaled $2.6 trillion in the first seven months of the year, a record for this period since the peak observed during the pandemic in 2021.

John Waldron pointed out that deals backed by private equity funds are accelerating, a phenomenon that Goldman Sachs is also seeing in its own deal book. "We are reaching a point where this engine will start to run at full speed," he said.

The Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts should also stimulate activity by reducing the cost of capital for businesses, he explained.

From a macroeconomic perspective, Waldron expressed confidence in economic fundamentals, particularly in the United States, while acknowledging that trade and geopolitical tensions are holding back the global economy. "I have to say that the US economy is showing extraordinary resilience. Despite everything it has been through, it continues to move forward with strength," he said.

However, he warned that the US budget situation is unsustainable in the long term. "In the medium-to-long term, it would be much better for the US economy and for the health of the system if we got our spending under control," he said.

A budget watchdog group said in August that the US federal deficit would be nearly $1 trillion higher over the next ten years than the January forecast by the Congressional Budget Office. This revision is related to tax measures, additional spending laws, and tariffs.

Finally, John Waldron expressed concern about potential barriers to hiring talent globally. The Trump administration plans to impose a one-time fee of $100,000 for new H-1B visas, which could impact financial institutions, which are heavy users of these visas, even though technology companies remain the primary beneficiaries.

data.title: Jaguar Land Rover Restarts Some IT Systems After Cyberattack
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping


Jaguar Land Rover has begun a gradual restart of its operations, with some IT systems back online following the recent cyberattack on the U.K. automaker.

The company, owned by India's Tata Motors, discovered a cyberattack late last month, forcing it to shut down its computer systems and halt production.

In a statement late Thursday it said it had informed suppliers and retail partners that sections of its digital network were back up and running, and that processing capacity for invoicing has been increased as it works to quickly clear the backlog of payments due to suppliers.

"The foundational work of our recovery program is firmly under way," it said.

The company, which manufacturers Land Rover, Jaguar and Range Rover models, has been forced to repeatedly extend the production shutdown over the last few weeks as it races to restart systems safely with the help of cybersecurity experts flown in from around the globe. It is also working with the U.K.'s National Cyber Security Centre--part of the country's GCHQ intelligence agency--and law enforcement to ensure it restarts in a safe and secure manner.

The company has said some data was compromised, having initially said there wasn't evidence of stolen customer information.

JLR's global parts logistics center, which supplies spare parts that service customers' vehicles, is now returning to full operations. In addition, the system used to process the registration of wholesale vehicles has been brought back online, which is key for improving cash flow.

"These are important initial steps," the company said.


Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-26-25 0311ET

data.title: Bmw is recalling 196,355 U.S. vehicles as a short circuit in the starter relay may increase the risk of a fire- Nhtsa
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) -

  • BMW of North America, LLC is recalling 196,355 U.S. vehicles - Nhtsa
  • BMW of North America, LLC is recalling some U.S. vehicles as a short circuit in the starter relay may increase the risk of a fire. - Nhtsa

data.title: India New Issue-Arugn Technologies to issue multiple tenor bonds, bankers say
data.description:
MUMBAI, Sept 26 (Reuters) - India's Arugn Technologies plans to raise 6 billion rupees
($67.6 million) through the sale of bonds maturing in 15 months and in four years, three bankers
said on Friday.
    It has invited coupon and commitment bids for the issues on Monday, and the four-year issue
has a put option at the end of three years, they said. 
    The company did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment. 
    
 
  Here is the list of deals reported so far on September 26:

data.title: Indonesia's rupiah will strengthen to reflect fundamentals, finance minister says
data.description:
Jakarta, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Indonesia's finance minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa on Friday said he believed the rupiah would strengthen to levels that reflect its fundamentals, with investors expected to return to Indonesian assets due to improving economic prospects.

The rupiah fell to its lowest level since April earlier on Friday, but has since regained some of the losses.

Reporting by Stefanno Sulaiman; Writing by Gayatri Suroyo; Editing by David Stanway

data.title: Gazprom completes maintenance of Power of Siberia gas pipeline to China
data.description:
Moscow, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Gazprom had completed scheduled maintenance of the Power of Siberia gas pipeline, which supplies Russian gas to China, the company said on Friday.

Maintenance was carried out from Sept. 18-26. (Reporting by Reuters; Writing by Lucy Papachristou; editing by Vladimir Soldatkin)

data.title: Reckitt Benckiser to launch second tranche of buyback scheme
data.description:
Reckitt Benckiser Group PLC - Slough, England-based consumer health and hygiene products, including Durex, Nurofen and Vanish - Plans to commence the second tranche, worth GB250 million, of its GBP1 billion buyback programme initiated in July. Intends to begin the new tranche following completion of the initial GBP250 million tranche, which it expects to complete in October. Adds that the second buyback tranche is expected to complete by January 30 next year. It will be run by BNP Paribas. Notes the buyback programme's purpose of increasing returns to shareholders and reducing Reckitt's share capital.

Current stock price: 5,552.00

12-month change: up 20%

By Christopher Ward, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

data.title: Aena carries out monitoring of possible decisions on management of airports
data.description:
Sept 26 (Reuters) - Aena SME SA:

  • With regard to statements by one of the co's shareholders on possible involvement of autonomous regions in the management of airports it has carried out detailed monitoring
  • Monitoring of any possible decisions in this area to assess their potential impact on the company
  • No political proposal, project or decision known to Aena, as a result of the monitoring carried out, has reached the level of maturity that would require action by co
  • Company to continue to monitor developments and, where necessary, will take the measures required to uphold the current law

(Gdansk Newsroom)

data.title: Biocause Pharma Delisted From Shenzhen Bourse
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Hubei Biocause Pharmaceutical's (SHE:000627) listing on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange was terminated, according to a Friday filing.

The Chinese pharmaceutical voluntarily applied for the listing's termination at the beginning of September.

data.title: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Declares Interim Dividend for Financial Year 2025-26; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Glenmark Pharmaceuticals' (NSE:GLENMARK, BOM:532296) board has approved an interim dividend of 2.50 Indian rupees per equity share of face value of 1 rupee each for the financial year 2025-26, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The record date to determine the eligible shareholders for the dividend has been fixed as Oct. 3, and it will be within 30 days.

The company's shares were down nearly 3% in recent trade.
